reasoning: The previous 360-day average for protests in BIOT was exactly 0.000. For the question to resolve positively, a protest would need to happen in the 30-day window preceding January 11, 2026 (Dec 12, 2025 to Jan 11, 2026) and February 3, 2026 (Jan 4, 2026 to Feb 3, 2026). Previously, the main source of protests in BIOT (which is essentially just a highly restricted US-UK military base with no permanent civilian population) was a group of roughly 60 Tamil asylum seekers who were detained there since 2021. However, in December 2024, the UK relocated all but 3 of these asylum seekers to the UK or other secure settings. With only three asylum seekers remaining, and intense military security restricting access to the territory, there is no one left to protest (ACLED defines a protest as 3 or more individuals, and it is highly implausible that the 3 remaining individuals, two of whom have criminal convictions, will mount a public protest). Additionally, access by Chagossians is tightly regulated through supervised heritage visits, mitigating risk of unauthorized protests. No activist flotillas (e.g. Greenpeace) have announced or are currently en route to the region. I therefore place the probability at the absolute minimum (0.02) for the near-term Jan 11 date, and slightly higher (0.03) for the Feb 3 date to allow for unexpected events entering the 30-day window.
